name: Aluminum
summary: CONFIRMED UNDER ATTACK: IRGC struck both ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum smelters on March 28-29, injuring 8 workers and causing significant damage. LME aluminum surged to $3,500/mt — four-year high. Gulf accounts for ~9% of global aluminum production. ALBA shut 19% of capacity (Lines 1-3). EGA's Al Taweelah (1.6M tonnes/year) sustained significant damage. Cascades to auto manufacturing, construction, aerospace, packaging. US producers surging as beneficiaries.

signals.content: IRGC claimed attacks on ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum facilities in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants. ALBA confirmed the attack — two employees injured. EGA's Al Taweelah site sustained significant damage, six people injured. ALBA had already initiated 'controlled and safe shutdown' of ~19% capacity (Lines 1-3) due to Hormuz-related disruptions. EGA's Al Taweelah produced 1.6 million tonnes of cast metal in 2025. Gulf aluminum output represents ~9% of global production. (Sources: The National Abu Dhabi, Al Jazeera, AlCircle, Business Standard, Gulf Business)

LME aluminum surged to $3,492-$3,500/mt — four-year high — following the ALBA/EGA attacks. Iranian attacks on UAE and Bahrain aluminum plants have heightened supply risks. The National reported aluminum prices 'likely to remain high' as long as Gulf disruption risks persist. This is a new category in Iran's targeting expansion: military → energy → industrial (aluminum). Aluminum is critical input for auto manufacturing, construction, aerospace, and packaging. India imports significant aluminum — cost pressure on Indian auto and construction sectors. (Sources: The National, LME data, Financial Content/MarketMinute)

edges.context: IRGC struck ALBA and EGA in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants. New industrial targeting category.
ALBA (Aluminium Bahrain) — 1.62M mt/year. 19% capacity already shut. Two workers injured in IRGC attack.
EGA (Emirates Global Aluminium) — Al Taweelah produced 1.6M mt in 2025. Significant damage from IRGC attack. Six injured.
India imports aluminum. LME at $3,500/mt increases input costs for Indian auto (Tata Motors, Maruti), construction, and packaging sectors.

trigger_points.condition: Gulf aluminum production drops >25% sustained (ALBA + EGA combined)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would significantly tighten global aluminum supply. LME could hit $4,000+. Indian auto and construction sectors face margin compression. Hindalco (India's largest aluminum company) could benefit as domestic producer but faces higher energy costs.
